title: Death and Rebirth and Confusion
wikitext:
[]: Death and Rebirth and Confusion             Quote Post by Number 44 » Sat May 01, 2010 9:40 pm Finally watched Death and Rebirth, thanks to all who chimed in on if it was worth a viewing. Death and Rebirth felt to me like [URL=http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/CliffsNotes]Cliff Notes[/URL] for Eva. It arranged certain events of the series previously separated by many episode into an order so that the meaning and parallels were now easily understood. I had previously taken EoE as an alternate telling of the TV ending. Until I saw Death. One of my issue is that Death incorporates dialog from TV's ending. Add to that the Cello rehearsal. At first the Cello rehearsal looks like it's simply show Shinji's dull and peaceful existence prior to the events of the series. That is until Asuka, Rei, and Kaworu show up. So is this part of instrumentality? Overall, it reminds me of how episode 25 starts, and you think Shinji's self reflection at the begging of the episode is part of instrumentality, only for it to be a few scenes later Shinji starts to lose his form and they state instrumentality had begun. Can anyone clear this up for me, or at least state their view?

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Sat May 01, 2010 9:58 pm http://forum.evageeks.org/viewtopic.php?t=2235

Article: Death and Rebirth and Confusion
[]: Death and Rebirth and Confusion             Quote Post by Number 44 » Sat May 01, 2010 9:40 pm Finally watched Death and Rebirth, thanks to all who chimed in on if it was worth a viewing. Death and Rebirth felt to me like [URL=http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/CliffsNotes]Cliff Notes[/URL] for Eva. It arranged certain events of the series previously separated by many episode into an order so that the meaning and parallels were now easily understood. I had previously taken EoE as an alternate telling of the TV ending. Until I saw Death. One of my issue is that Death incorporates dialog from TV's ending. Add to that the Cello rehearsal. At first the Cello rehearsal looks like it's simply show Shinji's dull and peaceful existence prior to the events of the series. That is until Asuka, Rei, and Kaworu show up. So is this part of instrumentality? Overall, it reminds me of how episode 25 starts, and you think Shinji's self reflection at the begging of the episode is part of instrumentality, only for it to be a few scenes later Shinji starts to lose his form and they state instrumentality had begun. Can anyone clear this up for me, or at least state their view?

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Sat May 01, 2010 9:58 pm http://forum.evageeks.org/viewtopic.php?t=2235
